root_cause: Approval gate deadlock (config says disabled, runtime enforces)
timeout_mechanism: Commands wait for approval that never arrives
impact: Blocked command execution, requires Web UI terminal workaround
recovery_pattern: Pivot to file operations, script packaging, Web UI terminal

error_message: Request timed out before a response was generated.
possible_causes: Complex response generation (large context, multiple tool calls)
impact: User receives timeout error instead of response
recovery_pattern: Retry, simplify request, increase timeout config

observation: Web UI terminal provides interactive approval while Telegram times out
evidence: Human directed to use Web UI terminal for approval-needing commands
root_cause_insight: Different client interfaces have different timeout/approval behaviors
training_implication: Internal agent should support multiple channels with consistent timeout handling

context: Using read/write/edit tools to bypass approval gate
observation: File operations via tools bypass approval entirely, avoiding timeout
evidence: All analysis, script creation, documentation done via file ops
security_implication: Tools are controlled, shell is unrestricted (security boundary)
limitation: Cannot execute commands, only create/read/modify files
training_implication: Internal agent should allow safe file ops without approval timeout
